focuses.label: Conchylien, die er dem Städtischen Museum für Naturkunde

„German pharmacist settled in the Cape in 1847 and subsequently established the Baziya mission among the Themby. Carl Zeyher, who befriended him as a new arrival to Cape Town, introduced him to botany and the circle of resident and visiting naturalists from Germany. In 1855, having decided to become a missionary, he gave up collecting and entrusted his specimens to Rudolph Roser, who eventually took them to Germany. A visit many years later from Russell Hallack to his mission in the Transkei persuaded him to resume botanical collecting and brought him into contact with Prof. MacOwan, to whom he sent specimens of many previously undescribed species. Disa baurii H. Bolus, Moraea baurii Baker, Albuca bauri Baker and Scilla baurii Baker are among the plants named in his honour.“

focuses.label: Petra Gebauer NKGörlitz, 3.2.2023:Wann genau die Schriften nach Görlitz gekommen sind, ist nicht ersichtlich. Sicher gibt es Hinweise im Archiv der Naturforschenden Gesellschaft zu Görlitz. Das ist aber erst teilweise erschlossen (in sporadischer ehrenamtlicher bzw. früherer ABM-Tätigkeit), und es gibt noch viel zu tun. Einige Korrespondenzen zwischen Wenck und Mitgliedern der Gesellschaft liegen vor, ich habe sie noch nicht näher angesehen.
